response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with an impressive web and full-stack track record, but the role demands deep, current C++/STL expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ work was a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His DLang low-latency and event-driven systems work is conceptually adjacent, but he shows no exposure to MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices, all of which are core to this position rather than nice-to-haves.
strengths: Real low-latency, concurrent systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Event-driven architecture and distributed systems experience via his geo-data platform and microservices work
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing record (workshops, courses, code katas), matching the role's mentorship expectations
concerns: Professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months over a decade ago; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js-centric
No evidence of industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), IEC 62443, or SDL practices
Career trajectory points toward web/full-stack and developer tooling, suggesting weak alignment with an on-prem industrial C++ role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IoT protocol background
